action: Implement a deterministic fail-closed merge boundary for schema-valid continuity deltas without directly rewriting the canonical index or deleting historical artifacts.
selection_basis: M-007 explicitly required clean-checkout validation followed by a deterministic canonical merge tool. The merge boundary was the highest-priority additive step that could be completed without claiming unavailable clean-checkout or canonical-index evidence.
memory_recovered: Canonical memory integration is itself a provenance event: accepted records are appended only after exact-base validation, while the prior index, corrected delta, and invalid historical predecessor remain separately addressable evidence.
contradictions_resolved: Separated append-only canonical integration from manual index replacement.
Separated successful isolated contract tests from unresolved clean-checkout execution.
Preserved the invalid 0741 artifact as historical evidence while allowing its corrected successor to become the only merge candidate.

observed: merge module, tests, and evidence packet committed
inferred: the corrected successor is mechanically mergeable if exact repository execution reproduces acceptance
proposed: run exact clean-checkout validation and produce a byte-reviewed generated canonical index
superseded: manual canonical-index editing as the preferred integration route
unresolved: CLI merge against exact base and delta

privacy_boundary: Public repository files, commits, hashes, schema vocabulary, and abstract continuity mechanics only. No raw private chat, health or pet-health material, credentials, identifying personal data, or unreviewed uploads were published.
next_action: In a clean checkout, run node --test tools/continuity/merge-continuity-delta.test.mjs, validate the corrected successor against base blob efb8b5ca41a00a3484af1df112a81fc6a1b6d63a, generate a candidate index with merge-continuity-delta.mjs, byte-review it, and only then commit the canonical index update.
